channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Este es el momento ideal para comprar un vehículo eléctrico
item.description: Las energías limpias son cada vez más accesibles, pero aún no asumimos colectivamente el cambio necesario.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grunwald
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Should These Abandoned Tracks Become a Park or a Train Line? Or Both?
item.description: A disused rail line in Queens could help reconnect the borough and add much-needed park space. A new report suggests that a plan to do both is possible, but it faces long odds.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A stretch of abandoned train tracks in central Queens is currently set to be turned into a park.

item.title: Picture Prompt: BTS’s Comeback
item.description: Are you celebrating the band’s return? What other artists on hiatus would you like to see make a comeback?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times

item.title: What to Know About Alpha-Gal Syndrome, a Meat Allergy Linked to Ticks
item.description: If you’re having an unexplained, intense reaction after eating beef or pork, it’s a good idea to seek a blood test.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Johnny Milano for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ticks are flourishing because of the resurgence of the white-tailed deer, the tick’s most important host.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 25, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: It Begins as a Tick Bite and Can Be Devastating. And It’s Spreading.
item.description: The incidence of alpha-gal syndrome appears to be growing significantly. Patients who are bitten can develop a severe allergy to red meat, and a few have died.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Elizabeth Cecil for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Most cases of alpha-gal syndrome are believed to start with a bite from the lone star tick.

item.title: Mamdani Is Quietly Backing Away From a Threat to Raise Property Taxes
item.description: The mayor has left lawmakers with the belief he does not plan to pursue an unpopular property tax increase, as he looks to close a budget gap of $5.4 billion over two years.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg, Benjamin Oreskes and Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani blamed his predecessor, Eric Adams, for leaving the city with a multibillion-dollar deficit.

item.title: Write It Up! How Fast Do Our Journalists Type?
item.description: When it comes to breaking news, it’s a race not only to get the story, but to record it. We tracked down some of the speediest fingers in our newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: Youth Program Hit by $17 Million Scam Had Prior Misconduct, Records Show
item.description: New York City officials still have not fully explained how payroll cards from a summer jobs program were used to withdraw large amounts of cash from A.T.M.s last year.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan

media:content.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘litigation’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 1099 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: El tango ayuda a las personas con párkinson en Buenos Aires
item.description: Una vez a la semana, los pacientes de un hospital argentino utilizan los movimientos de su apasionado baile nacional para tratar problemas de equilibrio, rigidez y coordinación.
item.dc:creator: Magalí Druscovich and Pam Belluck

item.title: El verdadero valor de los zorros voladores
item.description: Australia trataba a estos grandes murciélagos frugívoros como plaga, pero investigaciones indican que aportan cientos de millones de dólares en beneficios a la economía del país.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ham

item.title: Estados Unidos se ha convertido en una nación peligrosa
item.description: Estados Unidos ya no es el líder del mundo libre.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Lozada
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Naila Ruechel para The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: litigation
item.description: This word has appeared in 1,099 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Late Night Questions the ‘Very Big Present’ Trump Got From Iran
item.description: Josh Johnson was puzzled by what kind of gift the president could have received “from the people you are currently at war with.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: For Putin, the War in Iran Changed Everything
item.description: In one swoop, it overturned the conditions for conciliation.
item.dc:creator: Mikhail Zygar

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Alexander Zemlianichenko

item.title: Base en la Luna y nave nuclear a Marte: el nuevo plan de la NASA
item.description: La agencia fija metas y plazos concretos para impulsar la exploración espacial y recuperar la confianza en su misión.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:credit: John Raoux/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: El sistema de cohetes Artemis II de la NASA al amanecer del martes. Entre otros objetivos, la agencia anunció planes para acelerar el ritmo de las misiones Artemis, de una cada tantos años a dos al año.

item.title: Why a Strong Butt Is Key to Healthy Aging
item.description: The benefits of a strong butt go beyond just aesthetics. A robust body of research shows that strengthening your glutes is important to healthy aging and longevity. Erik Vance, a senior staff editor for Well, explains.
item.dc:creator: Erik Vance and Theodore Tae

item.title: Corea del Sur pide duchas más cortas ante crisis energética por la guerra con Irán
item.description: El presidente Lee Jae Myung llamó a la cooperación ciudadana, comparando la crisis de suministro con la financiera de los 90 y la pandemia.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Anthony Wallace/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El gobierno de Corea del Sur está alentando a la gente a utilizar el transporte público para ayudar a ahorrar energía.

item.title: A Missile Fragment in a Schoolyard
item.description: Examining an image of children near a missile fragment, which landed near a school as several families were sheltering inside it.
item.dc:creator: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: In One Image A Missile Fragment in a Schoolyard by Avishag Shaar-Yashuv

item.title: Sarah Mullally, the First Female Archbishop of Canterbury, is About to Be Enthroned
item.description: Sarah Mullally will be installed on Wednesday at Canterbury Cathedral. Her appointment to the role has been both celebrated, and denounced by some factions within the global Anglican Church.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Jeff Moore
media:content.media:description: The ceremony in Canterbury on Wednesday is the symbolic start of Sarah Mullally’s ministry, after she was legally confirmed as archbishop at St. Paul’s Cathedral in London in January.

item.title: Gambia Says the Island Is Cursed. Migrants Saw an Opportunity.
item.description: Thousands of African migrants hoping to reach Europe have flocked to a remote island in Gambia that local villagers say is protected by a curse.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Muhamadou Bittaye

media:content.media:description: A damaged wooden pirogue used by migrants rests along the shore of Jinack Island, in Gambia, on Sunday.

item.title: Corrections: March 25, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, March 25, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Hunting for Meteorites in the Name of Science (and Cash)
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, March 25, 2026.

item.title: LaGuardia Crash Timeline: Moments Before Air Canada Plane Collided With Fire Truck
item.description: Air traffic audio and a detailed timeline from federal investigators reveal the final moments before a jetliner collided with a fire truck.
item.dc:creator: Jan Ransom, Bora Erden, Raj Saha and Mika Gröndahl
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Massachusetts Teacher Charged With Raping Two Students
item.description: The students say the former teacher at Miss Hall’s, a boarding school, groomed them and began having sex with them at age 16, which state law considers old enough for consent.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell

media:content.media:credit: David Degner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Matthew Rutledge, who was indicted on three counts of rape on Tuesday, taught at Miss Hall’s school in Pittsfield, Mass.

item.title: Somali Immigrants Have Effectively Been Denied a Fair Hearing, Lawsuit Says
item.description: Two Minnesota-based legal groups accused the Justice Department of unconstitutionally expediting Somalis’ immigration cases.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The vast majority of the 80,000 Somalis who live in Minnesota are American citizens or legal permanent residents.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 25, 2026
item.description: Don’t think twice — or do? — about solving Jeff Stillman’s crossword.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Oil Prices Tumble as Trump Shows Eagerness to Talk to Iran
item.description: Investors reacted to signals of possible de-escalation of the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times

item.title: Plan to Disarm Hamas in Gaza Is Detailed at U.N.
item.description: A Board of Peace member said the most dangerous weapons would be collected first. He linked compliance with disarmament to reconstruction beginning in the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hamas fighters in Gaza City in November.

item.title: 2,000 U.S. Troops From 82nd Airborne Division to Be Sent to Middle East
item.description: The order gives President Trump more military options as he considers diplomacy with Iran. It is unclear where the soldiers will go in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Members of the 82nd Airborne Division in North Carolina in 2021. It is unclear where roughly 2,000 soldiers from the division will go in the Middle East, but the location would be within striking distance of Iran, officials said.

item.title: Iran Says ‘Non-Hostile’ Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Cargo ships in the Gulf, near the Strait of Hormuz, seen from the United Arab Emirates earlier this month.

item.title: T.S.A. Tipped Off ICE Agents Before Arrests at San Francisco Airport
item.description: Transportation Security Administration officials told ICE that a mother and daughter under a detention order had planned to fly domestically, federal documents show.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Jeff Chiu/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Angelina Lopez-Jimenez and her 9-year-old daughter were detained at the San Francisco International Airport and later deported to Guatemala.

item.title: LaGuardia Crash Tests New Port Authority Leader
item.description: Kathryn Garcia, who began leading the agency in February, consoled two injured firefighters and dealt with delays at the three big airports serving New York City.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kathryn Garcia, the executive director of the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, met with two employees who were injured in the collision.

item.title: Democrat Emily Gregory Wins Florida Special Election in Mar-a-Lago’s District
item.description: Emily Gregory’s victory in Palm Beach brought the Democratic surge to President Trump’s backyard, while a union leader leads in a race for a state senate seat vacated by Florida’s lieutenant governor.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen

media:content.media:credit: Josh Ritchie for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emily Gregory’s district includes President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Fla.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The U.S. was said to have sent Iran a peace plan via Pakistan on a day that the Iranians fired a torrent of missiles across the region.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Mati Hashemi/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Emergency workers inspecting the site of a strike in a residential area of the northwestern city of Tabriz, Iran, on Tuesday.

item.title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Halt Iran War Without Authorization
item.description: Senate Democrats failed for the third time to advance a resolution that would force the removal of troops from hostilities in Iran unless Congress approves offensive operations.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Christopher S. Murphy, Democrat of Connecticut, introduced Tuesday’s war powers resolution.

item.title: After Standing Up to Trump Over Greenland, Denmark’s Prime Minister Leads in Election
item.description: Mette Frederiksen’s party fell far short of a majority, but analysts say she is still in the best position to form a new government.
item.dc:creator: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman

media:content.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Denmark’s prime minister, Mette Frederiksen. Her party came in first in Tuesday’s election, but won less than a quarter of the vote.

item.title: Driver Hops Curb Outside Iowa School, Injuring 9 Students
item.description: The injured students were taken to local hospitals after the crash, which the authorities said did not appear to be intentional.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

item.title: Someone Left Their Heart at Grand Central, Captivating Commuters
item.description: A heart-shaped balloon appeared on the ceiling of the main concourse around Valentine’s Day, giving rise to delight and theories about its origins.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Michelle V. Agins/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A red, heart-shaped balloon above the main concourse of Grand Central Terminal on Tuesday.

item.title: Flooding in Hawaii: What We Know
item.description: There were no deaths reported, but hundreds of people were rescued and thousands evacuated as a series of hard-hitting storms inundated the islands over the last two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The flooding was largely caused by back-to-back storms that forecasters call a Kona low.

item.title: Maryland’s Supreme Court Strikes Down Baltimore’s Climate Lawsuit
item.description: The decision represents a setback to other local governments around the country that have sued oil companies to recoup the mounting costs of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick

media:content.media:credit: Stephanie Scarbrough/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Crude oil storage in Baltimore. The governments of Baltimore, Annapolis and Anne Arundel County had sued 26 oil and gas companies to recover damages caused by greenhouse gas emissions.

item.title: TSA Airport Delays: What It’s Like Waiting in Line for Hours
item.description: Scenes from extra long lines at two airports, and a surprising discovery at a third.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder, Shannon Sims and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport employees directing passengers in line at a Transportation Security Administration checkpoint on Monday.

item.title: Flight 8646 to LaGuardia: From Routine Landing to Disaster in 20 Seconds
item.description: In the early stages of the investigation into the Air Canada Express crash on Sunday night, a hazy timeline has begun to emerge.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Days after the collision with a fire truck on the runway, Air Canada Flight 8646 remains on the tarmac at LaGuardia Airport.

item.title: OpenAI Is Shutting Down Sora, Its A.I. Video Generator
item.description: The start-up said it would discontinue Sora just three months after signing a multiyear deal to bring Disney characters to the service.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

item.title: New York Times Accuses Pentagon of Defying Court Order
item.description: The company said in a legal filing that the department sought to fashion an “end run” when it issued revised media rules on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York Times accused the Pentagon of adopting a fresh round of media restrictions in retaliation for an adverse ruling from a federal judge last week.

item.title: LaGuardia Crash Survivors Recount Ordeal
item.description: Survivors of an Air Canada Express flight that crashed into a fire truck at LaGuardia Airport on Sunday evening recalled details of the terrifying incident.
item.dc:creator: Monika Cvorak

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Passengers on the Air Canada flight that crashed into a truck at LaGuardia Airport did not know what had happened to their plane, but knew they had to evacuate quickly.

item.title: The Aching Power of Abraham Vazquez
item.description: Our critic Jon Caramanica breaks down Abraham Vazquez’s “Se Apagó la Luz,” a startlingly beautiful song about loss that will kick you in the teeth.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli

item.title: Meta Ordered to Pay $375 Million Over Child Safety Violations
item.description: In one of the company’s first major losses, New Mexico jurors found that it had misled consumers about the safety of its platforms, enabling sexual exploitation of young users.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang and Eli Tan

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Meta, which is led by Mark Zuckerberg, said it would appeal a verdict in state court in Santa Fe, N.M.

item.title: Costa Rica acepta acoger a migrantes deportados por el gobierno de Trump
item.description: El acuerdo forma parte de los esfuerzos del presidente Trump por encontrar gobiernos dispuestos a aceptar a personas que fueron detenidas en Estados Unidos.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Anna Moneymaker
media:content.media:description: La entonces secretaria de Seguridad Nacional, Kristi Noem, con el presidente de Costa Rica, Rodrigo Chaves Robles, durante una rueda de prensa el año pasado en San José, Costa Rica.
